description: Displays the total value of all scheduled recurring, scheduled one-time, and manual one-time charges and credits included in Effective Rent amortized over the occupied length of the current lease interval. Charge Codes can be included/excluded from Effective Rent under Setup >> Company >> Financial >> Charge Codes >> [Select Charge Code] >> Include in Effective Rent. Occupied lease length is the best approximation of when scheduled charges start and end on a lease interval and best accounts for early move-ins, early move-outs, and transfers. description: Displays the average Current Lease Term (Months) for the units. For integer-based lease terms (i.e. conventional), this value is the Lease Term converted to a simple integer. description: Displays the average Current Occupied Lease Length for the units. Current Occupied Lease Length is the number of months in the Current Lease interval between earliest of Move-in and Lease Start and earliest of Move-out and Lease End. This value is calculated by dividing the number of days between earliest of Move-in and Lease Lease Start and earliest of Move-out and Lease End by the number of days per month in an average year. description: Displays the average Current One-time Concessions for the units. description: Displays the average Current Recurring Concessions for the units. description: ASSUMED Occupancy PERCENTAGE as of 30 days after the "Week Ending" date. This is calculated by taking the count of all Occupied units as of the "Week Ending" date, ADDING the scheduled move-ins, and SUBTRACTING the scheduled move-outs in the 30 day period after the "Week Ending" date. This number is then DIVIDED BY the count of Rentable units as of the "Week Ending" period. VALIDATE this column by calculating occupancy percentage using the move-ins and move-outs shown in the Trending Occupancy v1.1 when: 1. Period: Custom Date (week ending date) 2. Future Periods: 4 3. Excluded Units: Hide To calculate the "30 Day Trend" take the BEGIN OCCUPIED UNITS value as of the week ending date, ADD the move-ins which occurred between the week ending date and 30 future days, and SUBTRACT the move-outs which occurred between the week ending date and 30 future days. Note: You may need to drill-down into the values in the Trending Occupancy v1.1 to see if the move-ins and move-outs listed should be included in the date range.

description: Displays the average Current Net Effective Rent for the units. Current Net Effective Rent is the total value of all scheduled recurring, scheduled one-time, and manual one-time charges and credits included in Effective Rent amortized over the occupied length of the current lease interval. Charge Codes can be included/excluded from Effective Rent under Setup >> Company >> Financial >> Charge Codes >> [Select Charge Code] >> Include in Effective Rent. Occupied lease length is the best approximation of when scheduled charges start and end on a lease interval and best accounts for early move-ins, early move-outs, and transfers. Month-to-month lease intervals do not have scheduled recurring charges total value or occupied lease length calculated and therefore, are calculated as the sum of active (at the end of the lease for lease intervals in Past status) recurring Scheduled Charges with Charge Codes included in Effective Rent.

description: The Month(s) column will display the average occupancy percentage for each listed month based on the grouping. The occupancy percentage should represent the percent of all rentable units that are occupied. Past Month - The percentage will be based on the last day of the month (taking into consideration the number of vacant days in that full month) Current Month - The percentage will be based on the current calendar date (taking into consideration the number of vacant days as of the current date) Future Month - This percentage will be based on the current percentage (taking into consideration the number of vacant days as of the current date) but ALSO factoring in future renewals, move-ins, and move-outs . This will also take into account the Future residents who do not have units assigned to them.
